ingredients.title: Ingredients (Serves 4–6)
items: 1 kg (2.2 lbs) boneless chicken thighs (juicier) or breast

ingredients.title: For the Marinade:
items: ¼ tsp ground cardamom (optional, for authentic aroma)
1 tsp chili powder (optional, adjust to taste)

ingredients.title: Optional Aromatics:
items: 2 tbsp white vinegar or apple cider vinegar

instruction.title: Marinate the Chicken
steps: Slice chicken thighs into thin strips (or leave whole if you’ll slice after cooking).
In a large bowl, combine garlic, spices, yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, onion, and vinegar.
Add chicken and coat well. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight. This ensures maximum flavor infusion.

instruction.title: Cooking – Traditional or Pan
Option A: Oven (best for even cooking)
steps: Place chicken on a lined baking sheet or roasting pan.
Roast for 20–25 minutes, turning halfway, until fully cooked and slightly charred on edges.
instruction.title: Option B: Skillet / Grill
steps: Heat a non-stick pan or grill over medium-high heat.
Cook chicken in batches, 4–5 minutes per side, until golden brown and cooked through.
Optional: After cooking, broil for 2–3 minutes to get a charred, restaurant-style look.

instruction.title: Serving
steps: Slice the chicken thinly if not already done.
Serve wrapped in pita with garlic sauce, pickles, fresh veggies, and a sprinkle of parsley.
Can also serve over rice for a plate-style shawarma.

chefTips: Chicken Choice: Thighs are juicier and more forgiving than breast.
Marination: Overnight marination is key for tender, flavorful shawarma.
Char Flavor: Finish under the broiler or on a hot skillet for authentic restaurant char.
Sauce: Homemade garlic sauce elevates the dish dramatically.
